mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0, or the signal loses predictive power after 2016.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from construction to manufacturing, boosting corporate profit margins and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative direction-accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or INDPRO and HOUST diverge without SPX reacting.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, a simultaneous contraction in real consumer credit forces deleveraging, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite falling credit, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly increase in real GDP growth rate expands aggregate demand, directly lifting corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC stays below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or quarterly GDP growth turns negative while SPX 20-day returns remain positive in over 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 100, supply chain capacity utilization peaks, leading to margin compression from input cost inflation and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO crosses 100, or the signal fails to predict margin compression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth paired with a federal funds rate above 2.0 percent signals an overheating economy where the Fed will aggressively hike rates, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a direction-accuracy below 45% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or SPX returns remain positive despite the overheating configuration.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained rise in housing starts combined with falling unemployment accelerates consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in over 55% of triggered instances, or the housing-labor interaction loses predictive power after 2018.
